# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Docker Compose file for ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) and APM Server. Configure all services with network settings. Set up communication between components.
# Category: monitoring_misconfiguration

version: '3' # Version of the docker-compose format to use (default is v2).  Use version 3 if you are using Docker Compose V1 or later versions and only one service in your compose file has a single port. For services with multiple ports, this option needs explicitly set as true
services: # Services defined by the user will be placed here under either 'version' key (for v2) OR simply below it for Single-Service Compatibility mode that uses version 3 and only one service in your compose file has a single port. Use different labels or names to reference them later as well
    elasticsearch: # ElasticSearch Service configuration here, set appropriate values accordingly based on requirement like network_mode etc... For instance if you are using the default Docker images for each component then this is not needed but can be used otherwise use a custom image.  Include all necessary environment variables and volumes to configure your service according requirements
        image: elasticsearch:7.8.0 # ElasticSearch Image or Use Custom Images (Only if you have customized the default ones) Here we are using latest version of ELK stack, replace with suitable versions based on requirement like 6/7 for particular component only ect... Also include necessary ports as per requirements
        network_mode: elasticsearch-network # Set up a custom Network or use Docker's built in bridge mode if you want all components to be within same Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) and also remember, ElasticSearch is not directly reachable from outside the container. You need Nodes of Kibana/Logstash service running on this network
        volumes: # Setup necessary persistent data storage here as per requirement for ELS logs & Metadata files ect... For instance you can use a Docker volume to hold these log or metadata related file(s).  Remember, the Log and Warehouse are not directly reachable from outside of their respective containers.
        environment: # Setup Environment Variables here as per requirements (Also remember ElasticSearch is an immutable system so we need specific variables set) ect... For instance you can use a variable to keep logstash container active or otherwise if necessary, it will listen for requests on this port(number).
    kibana: # KIBANA Service configuration here (Like elasticsearch but used in case of UI related tasks like logs etc.) Similar steps as before with respecting your need and suitability to customize the image. Remember setting up a custom network_mode or using Docker's built-in bridge mode if necessary, also make sure that no external applications are connected via default port for communication between Elasticsearch (5601) & Kibana containers
    logstash: # LogStash Service configuration here with respecting requirement. You need to set up volume and environment variables according requirements too like setting the required ports, volumes etc... Also remember this service is not directly reachable from outside of their respective container's network hence setup a custom_network (ElasticSearch-Network)
    apm: # APM Server Service configuration here. Follow suit with requirement or customize as per your need using appropriate image/service and environment variables required for it based on requirements ect... Remember all services should be able to communicate within the same network only, no other service needed outside of their respective container's networks
        image: elastic/apm-server # Use a custom APM server Image or select suitable ones (based requirement) like 5.0 for specific component and so on ... Also include all required environment variables inside this part as per requirements if any . Remember, once the service is started up it should be reachable via appropriate ports provided during setup
        network_mode: elasticsearch-network # Setup a custom Network or use Docker's built in bridge mode  If you wish your services to reside within same VPC and communicate with each other only over this private cloud (VPC) then provide that information here. This setting ensures communication between APM Server & Kibana service via Logstash, ElasticSearch is not reachable from outside of its container network
        environment: # Setup Environment Variables required for the Apm server inside Here like PROMETHEUS_INTERNAL or any other variable if needed. For instance you can use a port to listen and connect Prometheus metrics collection, Logstash logs etc ... Also remember this service should not be directly reachable from outside of it's container network
        ports: # Setup necessary Ports here as per requirement ect... Remember Kibana/LogStash & APM server are running on different containers hence they need access to each other via these respective services. Port mapping must match this for all communication within the stack (ELK Stack) and between ElasticSearch, Logstash service
            - "5601:5601" # Map ELK ports here if required else use default values as per requirement ect... Also note that APM server only requires http_port to be reachable outside of its container network hence the map should remain same for it. If needed, other than these two port mappings you can have more
